info.title: Thor Trade Copier API
info.description: Programmatic access to Thor Trade Copier functionality. Manage connections, accounts, positions, and risk settings via REST API.

XCustomerId.name: X-Customer-Id
XCustomerId.description: Target customer ID for master API keys. Required for most endpoints when using a master key. Omit to use aggregated mode on supported endpoints (e.g., GET /positions).

availableAccounts.description: Trading accounts available under this connection (use AccountId to match with positions)

AccountId.description: Broker account number (matches AccountId in positions)

ConnectionCreate.description:
⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** After creating/updating/deleting connections, you MUST call `POST /api/v1/config/save-connections` to apply your changes and trigger the connection process.

OAuth-based vendors (Tradovate, cTrader) are NOT supported via API. Use the Thor dashboard for OAuth connections.

vendorName.description: Vendor name (see /api/v1/vendors for list). OAuth vendors (Tradovate, cTrader) are NOT supported.

accountId.description: Account ID / Username / Email for the vendor

server.description: Server address (required for Rithmic-based vendors and prop firms)

selectedConnection.description: Connection type: Demo, Live, or Real

customName.description: Custom display name for this connection

groupName.description: Group name for organization (defaults to vendorName)

dxFeed (MyFundedFutures).summary: dxFeed connection for MyFundedFutures - no server needed

dxFeed (Phoenix Trader Funding).summary: dxFeed connection for Phoenix Trader Funding - no server needed

ConnectionUpdate.description: ⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** After updating connections, you MUST call `POST /api/v1/config/save-connections` to apply your changes.

stopLoss.description: Daily stop loss limit (negative value, e.g., -1000)

takeProfit.description: Daily take profit goal (positive value, e.g., 1000)

AccountCreate.description: ⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** After creating/updating/deleting account configurations, you MUST call `POST /api/v1/config/save-accounts` to apply your changes.

phoenixId.description: Leader account ID (the account to copy FROM) - get this from GET /api/v1/connections

convertedId.description: Follower account ID (the account to copy TO) - get this from GET /api/v1/connections

stopLoss.description: Daily stop loss limit in dollars, must be negative (default: -1000)

takeProfit.description: Daily take profit goal in dollars, must be positive (default: 1000)

AccountUpdate.description: ⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** After updating account configurations, you MUST call `POST /api/v1/config/save-accounts` to apply your changes.

userId.description: Thor connection ID (e.g. user_1) - maps to connections endpoint

unlockTime.description: Unix timestamp (ms) when the lock expires

lockedAt.description: Unix timestamp (ms) when the lock was created

accountIds.description: Array of account IDs to lock

enabled.description: Whether news restrictions are enabled for this connection

restrictedEvents.description: List of news events to restrict (e.g., FOMC, NFP, CPI)

timeBeforeMinutes.description: Minutes before news to pause trading

notifyBeforeMinutes.description: Minutes before restriction to send notification

includeUSOpen.description: Include US market open in restrictions

includeUKOpen.description: Include UK market open in restrictions

includeAsiaOpen.description: Include Asia market open in restrictions

marketOpenAfterMinutes.description: Minutes after market open to resume trading

restrictionEndTime.description: ISO timestamp when current restriction ends

marketOpenAfterMinutes.description: Minutes after market open to resume

ExitPrice.description: Exit fill price (null if still open)

StopLoss.description: Stop loss price (null if not set)

TakeProfit.description: Take profit price (null if not set)

LowestURPnL.description: Maximum Adverse Excursion - worst unrealized P&L during the trade

HighestURPnL.description: Maximum Favorable Excursion - best unrealized P&L during the trade

IsJournaled.description: Whether the trade has journal entries

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API credentials

Forbidden.description: Forbidden - Active Journal or Thor subscription required

NotFound.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

RateLimited.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded (100 req/min)

ServiceUnavailable.description: Service Unavailable - Journal database is temporarily unavailable

get.description: Retrieve all trading connections for the authenticated user. Each connection includes a unique `userId` field that you can use with PUT and DELETE endpoints.

post.description:
Add a new trading connection. Returns the `userId` which can be used to update or delete the connection.

⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** After creating connection(s), you MUST call `POST /api/v1/config/save-connections` to apply your changes.

dxFeed (Phoenix Trader Funding).summary: dxFeed connection for Phoenix Trader Funding

userId.description: Unique connection ID - use this for PUT/DELETE operations

example.message: Connection created successfully. Use POST /api/v1/config/save-accounts to activate.
A connection with this accountId and vendor already exists

put.description:
Update an existing connection. Get the connection ID from GET /api/v1/connections or from the POST response when creating.

⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** After updating connection(s), you MUST call `POST /api/v1/config/save-connections` to apply your changes.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Connection ID (userId) - obtained from GET /connections or POST /connections response

delete.description:
Remove a trading connection. Get the connection ID from GET /api/v1/connections.

⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** After deleting connection(s), you MUST call `POST /api/v1/config/save-connections` to apply your changes.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Connection ID (userId) - obtained from GET /connections or POST /connections response

get.description: Retrieve all leader->follower copy configurations. Each configuration includes a unique `clientId` that you can use with PUT and DELETE endpoints.

post.description:
Create a new leader->follower copy configuration. Returns a `clientId` which can be used to update or delete the configuration.

⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** After creating configuration(s), you MUST call `POST /api/v1/config/save-accounts` to apply your changes.

clientId.description: Unique configuration ID - use this for PUT/DELETE operations

example.message: Account configuration created successfully. Use this clientId to update or delete the configuration.
A configuration with this leader and follower already exists

put.description:
Update an existing account configuration. Get the configuration ID from GET /api/v1/accounts or from the POST response when creating.

⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** After updating configuration(s), you MUST call `POST /api/v1/config/save-accounts` to apply your changes.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Configuration ID (clientId) - obtained from GET /accounts or POST /accounts response

delete.description:
Remove an account configuration. Get the configuration ID from GET /api/v1/accounts.

⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** After deleting configuration(s), you MUST call `POST /api/v1/config/save-accounts` to apply your changes.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Configuration ID (clientId) - obtained from GET /accounts or POST /accounts response

get.description: Retrieve all current open positions for today. This data resets daily and only contains the current trading day. For historical trade data, use GET /api/v1/historical/trades.
Retrieve all pending/working orders for today. This data resets daily.
Retrieve positions closed during the current trading day. This data resets daily. For full historical trade data, use GET /api/v1/historical/trades.
Retrieve only currently open (running) positions. Equivalent to filtering /api/v1/positions for entries where IsClosed is false. This data resets daily.

post.description: Flatten (close) all open positions across all accounts. This is an all-or-nothing operation. Only affects today's active positions.

get.description: Get all currently locked accounts. Locked accounts cannot trade until the lock expires or is manually removed.

post.description: Lock one or more accounts for a specified duration. This disables all trading configurations using these accounts.

delete.description: Unlock one or more accounts before their lock expires

accountIds.description: Array of account IDs to unlock

get.description: Get all news trading restrictions configured for your connections. News restrictions automatically pause trading around high-impact economic events.

put.description: Create or update news restriction for a connection. Common restrictedEvents: FOMC, NFP, CPI, PPI, GDP, Retail Sales, ISM Manufacturing

delete.description: Remove news restriction for a connection

post.description:
**IMPORTANT:** You MUST call this endpoint after making changes to connections (create, update, or delete). This triggers Thor to reload and apply your connection changes. Without calling this, your changes will be stored but not active.

**Workflow:**
1. Make your connection changes (POST/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/connections)
2. Call this endpoint to apply all changes at once

**IMPORTANT:** You MUST call this endpoint after making changes to account configurations (create, update, or delete). This triggers Thor to reload and apply your account configuration changes. Without calling this, your changes will be stored but not active.

**Workflow:**
1. Make your account config changes (POST/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/accounts)
2. Call this endpoint to apply all changes at once

Fetches the latest account list from your connected trading platforms.

**⚠️ ONLY use this after a reset of a newly purchased account.** This refreshes the available accounts from your trading platforms - it does NOT save any of your configurations.

**This endpoint does NOT save connections or account configurations.** Use `save-connections` or `save-accounts` for that.

example.message: Account sync from trading platforms triggered

post.summary: Get an OAuth URL to connect a trading account
post.description:
Returns a URL that your application opens as a popup. The user logs in on the vendor site, and after authentication, the popup redirects to your `redirectUri` with the access token as a query parameter.

**Supported vendors:** Tradovate, cTrader, Tradovate Live, cTrader Live

**Flow:**
1. Call this endpoint to get an `oauthUrl`
2. Open `oauthUrl` as a popup window
3. User logs in on the vendor site
4. Popup redirects to your `redirectUri` with `?vendor=...&access_token=...`
5. Use the access token to create a connection via `POST /api/v1/connections/oauth`

redirectUri.description: Your callback URL where the popup will be redirected with the access token

oauthUrl.description: URL to open as a popup for the user to authenticate

post.description:
Create a trading connection using an access token obtained from the OAuth flow.

**Important:** After creating the connection, you MUST call `POST /api/v1/config/save-connections` to activate it.

vendorName.description: The vendor from the OAuth flow

accessToken.description: The access token received from the OAuth callback redirect

accountName.description: Tradovate account name from the OAuth callback (used to build the account ID as TRDV_<name>)

customName.description: Optional custom name for this connection

example.message: Connection created. Call POST /api/v1/config/save-connections to activate.

get.description: Retrieve the list of supported trading platform vendors and their connection types.
Retrieve all unique trading accounts that have historical trade data. Requires an active Journal or Thor subscription.
Retrieve aggregated trade statistics including win rate, P&L, streaks, and daily performance. Supports filtering by date range, symbol, playbook, and account.
Retrieve the IDs of trades that have been journaled (have notes, tags, playbook, or rating). Useful for checking which trades have journal entries without fetching full trade data.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated trade IDs to check. If omitted, returns count of all journaled trades.

journaledIds.description: IDs that have journal entries (when ids param provided)

count.description: Total number of journaled trades (when ids param omitted)

get.description:
Retrieve historical trades with filtering and pagination. Supports cursor-based pagination for efficient traversal of large datasets, as well as offset-based pagination.

**Pagination:** Use `cursor` from the response `nextCursor` field for cursor-based pagination, or use `page` + `limit` for offset-based pagination. Cursor-based pagination is recommended for large datasets.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor for cursor-based pagination (from nextCursor in response)

total.description: Total matching trades (omitted with cursor pagination)

nextCursor.description: Cursor for next page (use with cursor param)

get.description: Returns a paginated list of customers across the master key's allowed instances. Requires a master API key.

403.description: Master key required or instance not allowed

get.description: Returns detailed information for a specific customer. Requires a master API key.

403.description: Master key required or instance not allowed

tags.name: Connections
tags.description: Manage trading platform connections. Remember to call save-connections after changes!

tags.name: Accounts
tags.description: Manage leader-follower copy configurations. Remember to call save-accounts after changes!

tags.name: Positions
tags.description: View and manage today's positions and orders. Position data resets daily. For historical trade data, use the Historical Trades endpoints.

tags.name: Risk Management
tags.description: Account lockouts and news trading restrictions

tags.name: Configuration
tags.description: Save changes and sync with trading platforms

tags.name: Vendors
tags.description: Available trading platform vendors and connection types

tags.name: Historical Trades
tags.description: Read-only access to historical trade data from the Journal database. Requires an active Journal or Thor subscription. For today's live positions, use the Positions endpoints.

tags.name: Customers
tags.description: List and inspect customers under a master API key.
